skill.name: cosmic-database
skill.description: Access COSMIC cancer mutation data with authenticated downloads, common file paths, and guidance for cancer genomics analysis workflows.
skill.summary: Download and use COSMIC cancer genomics datasets for research workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many alerts, but the external command and weak cryptography matches are false positives from markdown examples and COSMIC terminology. Confirmed behavior is an authenticated COSMIC downloader that makes network requests and writes selected files locally. No evidence found for prompt injection, hidden command execution, malware, or credential exfiltration beyond the intended COSMIC authentication flow.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Network Download Helper
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The script sends COSMIC credentials to the official COSMIC download endpoint and then downloads the URL returned by that service. This is intended functionality, but it handles sensitive credentials and remote data, so users should verify the endpoint and protect terminal history. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: Direct requests.get calls with auth parameters are present, and the surrounding code confirms legitimate COSMIC download intent rather than hidden exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct network requests with COSMIC credentials are present. Context shows intended authenticated data download, not malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Write
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The downloader writes remote content to a local output path chosen from the filepath basename or the command-line output option. This is expected for a download utility, but it can overwrite files if users provide unsafe output paths. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The open call writes binary data to output_filename, and the CLI exposes an output argument that controls that path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and user-controllable through output_filename. It is legitimate downloader behavior but has overwrite risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged markdown fences, inline code, Python examples, and command examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation and examples, not executable calls made by the skill. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The flagged locations are in SKILL.md or reference markdown, and the script contains no subprocess or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is documentation text and code examples. No executable shell invocation is present in the Python script.

low_findings.title: Official COSMIC URLs in Documentation and Script
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE, LOW RISK. The skill includes hardcoded COSMIC website and documentation URLs, plus the official file download endpoint. These URLs match the stated purpose and do not indicate suspicious third-party communication. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: Network references point to cancer.sanger.ac.uk or contact addresses documented as COSMIC resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and aligned with the skill description. No unrelated endpoint or covert destination was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The high-severity weak cryptography alerts occur on COSMIC descriptions, mutation terminology, or example text rather than cryptographic API usage. No hashing, encryption, or password storage code was found. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed flagged locations contain scientific documentation and downloader descriptions, not weak algorithms such as MD5 or SHA1 implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is domain terminology and documentation. There is no cryptographic operation in the reviewed code.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Requests with User Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: The script passes email and password credentials through requests.get auth when requesting COSMIC download URLs. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The code directly supplies auth=(email, password) to the official COSMIC endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential use is explicit. The endpoint is aligned with intended COSMIC authentication.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Content Written to Local Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script streams downloaded remote content into a local file selected by the caller. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The open call and chunk writes are direct, with output controlled by function or CLI arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is explicit and expected for a downloader. The remaining risk is accidental overwrite.

content.value_statement: Cancer researchers need reliable access to COSMIC files for mutation analysis. This skill provides download helpers, file path references, and workflow guidance.
actual_capabilities: Downloads COSMIC files through the authenticated COSMIC file download endpoint.
Maps common data types to COSMIC file paths for GRCh37 and GRCh38.
Documents core mutation, Cancer Gene Census, signature, fusion, and copy number files.
Provides Python and command-line examples for retrieving selected COSMIC datasets.
Explains common pandas and VCF workflows for downloaded COSMIC data.
limitations: Requires a valid COSMIC account and permission for the intended use.
Does not provide COSMIC data directly or bypass licensing requirements.
Large downloads require enough disk space and a stable network connection.
Does not validate downloaded dataset contents beyond HTTP status checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Cancer Gene Lists
use_cases.description: Download the Cancer Gene Census and use it to prioritize variants for a research project.

use_cases.title: Build Mutation Analysis Inputs
use_cases.description: Retrieve COSMIC mutation exports for integration into a variant annotation or filtering pipeline.

use_cases.title: Compare Data Releases
use_cases.description: Select specific COSMIC versions and genome assemblies for reproducible analysis across projects.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Which COSMIC file should I download for Cancer Gene Census data on GRCh38?

prompt_templates.title: Create a Download Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the command to download COSMIC coding mutations for GRCh38 using my COSMIC account email.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Pipeline Input
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose COSMIC files for a somatic variant annotation workflow that needs mutations, gene roles, and resistance data.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Reproducible Release Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible COSMIC download plan for GRCh37 and GRCh38, including version pinning, filenames, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: I need known cancer genes for variant prioritization.
output_examples.output: Use the Cancer Gene Census file. The response identifies the GRCh38 path, explains licensing needs, and suggests saving a versioned filename.

output_examples.input: I need mutation and resistance data for a pipeline.
output: Recommended files include the coding mutation export and resistance mutation export.
The guidance separates GRCh38 default paths from legacy GRCh37 paths.
It notes that downloaded files may be large and should be checked before pipeline ingestion.

output_examples.input: I want to analyze TP53 mutations from COSMIC.
output_examples.output: The skill points to the mutation export, recommends pandas filtering by gene name, and reminds the user to cite COSMIC.

best_practices: Use versioned output filenames when results must be reproducible.
Keep COSMIC credentials out of shared notebooks, logs, and shell history.
Confirm academic or commercial licensing before downloading restricted datasets.
anti_patterns: Do not publish downloaded COSMIC data without checking license terms.
Do not rely on latest paths for regulated or reproducible analyses.
Do not overwrite existing research files with unverified download output paths.

faq.question: Does this skill include COSMIC data?
faq.answer: No. It provides download helpers and documentation for users with valid COSMIC access.

faq.question: Do I need a COSMIC account?
faq.answer: Yes. COSMIC downloads require authentication, and commercial users may need a license.

faq.question: Which genome assemblies are supported?
faq.answer: The documented paths support GRCh38 and GRCh37, with GRCh38 used as the default.

faq.question: Can it download mutational signatures?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference includes the signatures path and explains common signature data types.

faq.question: Is this a clinical decision tool?
faq.answer: No. It supports research data access and does not provide medical advice or clinical validation.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
